summary: Matched four fighter functions in Kirby and Yoshi special-move code: Kirby SpecialAirHi2 animation transition, Kirby SpecialNLg motion-state setup, and Yoshi grounded/aerial SpecialHi enter routines. The PR replaced placeholder decomp markers with C implementations and reported all four as 100% matched.
what_changed: `ftKb_SpecialAirHi2_Anim` checks `ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining`; when the animation ends it changes to `ftKb_MS_SpecialAirHi3` with flags `0xA`, then installs `efLib_PauseAll` and `efLib_ResumeAll` as hitlag callbacks.

`ftKb_SpecialNLg_800F951C` calls `ftCommon_8007D5D4`, preserves `cur_anim_frame`, selects motion id `0x190` or `0x201` based on `fp2->fv.kb.hat.kind`, changes motion state with flags `0x5000`, and assigns `fn_800F9260` to `accessory4_cb`.
Implemented `ftYs_SpecialHi_Enter` for grounded Yoshi Egg Throw startup.
Implemented `ftYs_SpecialAirHi_Enter` for aerial Yoshi Egg Throw startup.

Both Yoshi enter functions reset `mv.ys.specialhi.x0`, `mv.ys.specialhi.x4`, `fv.ys.x2238`, `throw_flags`, and `cmd_vars[0]`, then enter the appropriate ground or air motion state, install `fn_8012E110` as `accessory4_cb`, and call `ftAnim_8006EBA4`.
smart_moves: The Yoshi grounded and aerial enter routines were matched as near-identical paired functions, differing only in the motion-state enum (`ftYs_MS_SpecialHi` versus `ftYs_MS_SpecialAirHi`). This preserves the common setup pattern while keeping the functions explicit.

The Kirby SpecialAirHi2 animation function uses the existing animation-completion helper and only installs hitlag pause/resume callbacks after transitioning into `ftKb_MS_SpecialAirHi3`, matching the behavior implied by the diff.
The Kirby SpecialNLg function preserves the current animation frame before changing motion state, avoiding an accidental animation restart when switching between copied/hat-dependent motion states.
The Yoshi functions include an explicit `u8 _[24];` stack-padding declaration, indicating a deliberate matching tactic for stack layout rather than a semantic gameplay variable.
decomp_lessons: For fighter enter-state routines, compare grounded and aerial variants together; they often share all initialization logic and differ only in the target motion-state ID.
When matching fighter motion transitions, preserve exact flag constants, start frame, animation rate, blend value, and callback assignments; these details are often necessary for a 100% match.
Do not remove seemingly unused stack padding if it is required for matching; this PR used `u8 _[24];` in both Yoshi enter functions.
Accessing fighter data may appear both as `gobj->user_data` and via `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` in the same function; preserve the observed sequence when it affects matching.

Hat/copy-state conditionals in Kirby code can select between numeric motion IDs before calling `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`; avoid over-renaming or abstracting these constants without stronger evidence.
naming_conventions: Matched functions keep existing project-style fighter prefixes: `ftKb_` for Kirby and `ftYs_` for Yoshi.
Motion-state enum names follow character and action naming such as `ftKb_MS_SpecialAirHi3`, `ftYs_MS_SpecialHi`, and `ftYs_MS_SpecialAirHi`.
Unidentified static helpers remain address-style names, e.g. `fn_800F98F4`, `fn_800F9260`, and `fn_8012E110`.
Unknown or partially understood fighter struct fields remain offset-style names such as `mv.ys.specialhi.x0`, `mv.ys.specialhi.x4`, and `fv.ys.x2238`.
Stack padding was named `_`, matching a common convention for intentional unused match-only local storage.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used explicit stack padding `u8 _[24];` in both Yoshi enter routines to satisfy stack-layout/codegen requirements.
Preserved reassignment from `gobj->user_data` to `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` after `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` in the Yoshi routines.
Stored `motion_id` and `anim_frame` locals in `ftKb_SpecialNLg_800F951C` before the motion-state call, likely matching register/local ordering in the target assembly.
Used literal flag constants `0xA` and `0x5000` in `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` calls where no named flag abstraction was introduced.
Matched callback writes directly to fighter fields: `pre_hitlag_cb`, `post_hitlag_cb`, and `accessory4_cb`.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The PR body stated that `ftKb_SpecialAirHi2_Anim`, `ftKb_SpecialNLg_800F951C`, `ftYs_SpecialHi_Enter`, and `ftYs_SpecialAirHi_Enter` were all 100% matched.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or document `fn_800F9260` or `fn_8012E110`.
Search for other Yoshi SpecialHi functions using `mv.ys.specialhi.x0`, `mv.ys.specialhi.x4`, or `fv.ys.x2238` to infer field meanings.
Search Kirby copy/hat logic around `fp->fv.kb.hat.kind` and motion IDs `0x190` and `0x201` for possible enum naming opportunities.
Compare other fighter enter routines that use `u8 _[24];` stack padding to identify recurring matching patterns.
